Caption: Peak 6 paraglider with Rocket P harness under experimental tests.

Normalised Peak 6 discretisation. Control points (blue crosses). Bound and trailing vortices associated with each panel (dashed cyan lines). Normal vectors (red arrows) located at the quarter-chord points (pink circles).
Force coefficient convergence as a function of HV panels for the Peak 6 canopy (under .. = 5° and β = 1°).
Influence of line length span ratio, on the canopy lift coefficient for Peak 6 with A R = 6.9.
S p l / b 2 ratio as a function of α and β angles in degrees.
Paraglider-harness/pilot frames and angles definition used along the 8 DOF model developed.
Longitudinal time response for speed system input: a) u C : x-components of the velocity vector over time; b) w C : z-components of the velocity vector; c) s s : speed system input.
Longitudinal time response for symmetric brake input. a) u C : x-components of the velocity vector over time; b) w C : z-components of the velocity vector; c) δ s : symmetric brake input.
3D system trajectory under asymmetric brake input in inertial frame.

Time response for an asymmetric brake input left to right manoeuvre; a) u C : x-components of the velocity vector over time; b) v C : y-components of the velocity vector; c) w C : z-components of the velocity; d) ϕ b : paraglider body roll angle; e) ψ b : paraglider body yaw angle; f) p b :paraglider body roll rate; g) r b :paraglider body yaw rate; h) ψ h relative yaw angle between harness/body and paraglider bodies and i) δ a : asymmetric brake input.
3D system trajectory under brake input right thermal manoeuvre in inertial frame.

Time response for asymmetric left brake input inside the thermal. a) u C : x-components of the velocity vector over time; b) v C : y-components of the velocity vector; c) w C : z-components of the velocity; d) δ a : asymmetric brake input.
